name: Toyota Human Support Robot (HSR)
short_description: A compact, omnidirectional mobile manipulator with a folding arm, designed to assist elderly and disabled people with everyday physical tasks at home.
long_description: The HSR is a lightweight, cylindrical wheeled robot with a single folding arm, an actuated head carrying RGB-D and stereo cameras, and a display for telepresence, built to fetch objects, pick items off the floor, and help with daily living. Since 2015 Toyota has distributed it to universities and labs worldwide as a shared open research platform running ROS, making it one of the most common testbeds for in-home service robotics and human-robot interaction. It matters for physical AI in daily life because it grounds embodied assistance research in a real, repeatable domestic robot rather than a simulation, letting many groups benchmark navigation, manipulation, and care tasks on the same hardware.

images.caption: Toyota Human Support Robot (HSR), showing its camera head, telepresence display, and folding gripper arm.
images.alt_text: A white cylindrical Toyota Human Support Robot with a sensor-laden head, a small display screen, and a black folding gripper arm reaching forward.

sources.title: Toyota Shifts Home Helper Robot R&D into High Gear with New Developer Community and Upgraded Prototype
